channel.title: Slow Route Journal
channel.description: Fresh takes for slow-travel and low-impact explorers.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your rail in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying rail project for slow-travel — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/onebag is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the local community this week.

item.title: The packing checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified packing purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of stays
item.description: The most common early stumbles in stays, and the cheap corrections that fix them for slow-travel and low-impact explorers.

item.title: "slow travel itinerary" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about slow travel itinerary through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: The state of Rail in 2026
item.description: A field report on rail: where things stand, and what slow-travel and low-impact explorers should do next.

item.title: Starter guide: Stays without the overwhelm
item.description: A practical on-ramp to stays for slow-travel and low-impact explorers — one repeatable workflow, no gear debt.

item.title: "train travel europe tips" — separating signal from noise
item.description: Thousands of people search "train travel europe tips" every month. Most of what they find is recycled. Here is what holds up.

item.title: Why slow-travel are rethinking Packing
item.description: A quiet shift is underway in how slow-travel and low-impact explorers approach packing — less maximalism, more maintenance.

item.title: Local on a budget: what actually matters
item.description: Where money changes outcomes in local — and the line items that are pure vanity.

item.title: What to watch next in Stays: five quiet shifts
item.description: Ignore the headlines — these are the slower currents reshaping stays for slow-travel and low-impact explorers.
